model_comparison: Model 1 demonstrates a much stronger adherence to the specific feature control command ('extremely slow speed') than Model 2. Model 1's audio is significantly elongated (approx. 1 char/sec), strictly following the instruction to slow down the speech to an extreme degree. Model 2, while producing a natural and content-complete audio, failed to execute the speed constraint, delivering the line at a relatively normal pace. Although Model 1 has a minor content defect (missing the final particle '了'), its performance on the core evaluation dimension (Speed) is superior because it actually attempted and succeeded in modifying the rate as requested.
